intro: APT41, CrystalX, and Fast16 were the most frequently observed threat actors in credential-theft incidents reported between April 12 and April 27, 2026, with CVE-2026-33829 emerging as the top exploited vulnerability. The 20 articles covered 14 high-severity and 4 critical-severity events, alongside one medium and one informational report. Technology, software development, cloud services, and enterprise sectors were primarily affected, with impacts noted across Global, Europe, North America, Bangladesh, and South Asia regions.
